title: Guidance Specification
description: Define boundaries, terms, non-goals, and feature decomposition for the next RevDeck iteration after v0.1 Binary Triage and M1 Plugin SDK preview.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Next iteration should pair user-visible TUI power navigation with adapter execution/commit depth; avoid full decompiler/debugger scope.

title: Product Manager Analysis
description: Prioritize user value, roadmap sequencing, and milestone shape.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Best next slice is Graph Lab plus Command Deck for immediate analyst value, while SDK M2 builds adapter runner and ObjectBatch commit behind it.

title: System Architect Analysis
description: Evaluate architecture, data model, plugin host evolution, and performance boundaries.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Host-mediated commits, provenance-rich plugin contributions, cached query view models, and background job records are the core technical enablers.

title: UI Designer Analysis
description: Design TUI surfaces, layout polish, visual hierarchy, and keyboard interaction improvements.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: TUI should add a first-class Graph Lab, Command Deck, richer Binary Map, status chips, breadcrumbs, and dense but stable visual hierarchy.

title: UX Expert Analysis
description: Assess analyst workflow, information architecture, and learning curve.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Reduce uncertainty with next-action queues, command previews, no-data diagnostics, evidence paths, and promote-to-finding workflows.

title: Test Strategist Analysis
description: Define regression, fixture, and acceptance strategy for the proposed features.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Use fixture-first adapters, snapshot-stable TUI tests, deterministic graph traversal tests, permission matrix tests, and large-list performance guards.

title: Synthesis And Feature Index
description: Resolve role findings into feature specs and a recommended milestone sequence.
topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
findings: Feature index contains 8 candidates; recommended M2A is TUI Power Navigation, M2B is Adapter Commit Pipeline.
